name: chrXIV_L_telomere
description: L-arm telomere of S. cerevisiae chromosome XIV. S. cerevisiae telomeres are tandem arrays of the degenerate TG(1-3) repeat (consensus TGTGGGTGTGGTG, average ~300 bp of array). Immediately interior lies the subtelomeric X element (core X plus optional X-combinatorial repeats) and, on some chromosome ends, 0-4 tandem copies of the Y' element (5.2 kb or 6.7 kb forms). sacCer3 represents most telomeres with ~300 bp of nominal TG(1-3) sequence at the very end.
expected_copy_count: ~75-150 copies of degenerate TG(1-3) (~300 bp array) per chromosome end; length is telomerase-regulated and strain-dependent
